tasks.title: Backend: eligible-aircraft query for a given route
tasks.description: Add a service function returning aircraft from my_fleet that are valid for a candidate route (origin hub, range >= distance, runway compatible at both endpoints, available capacity = owned_count - assigned_count > 0).
tasks.details: Location: app/services/fleet_service.py (new function get_eligible_aircraft(hub_iata, dest_iata, distance_km)). Join my_fleet -> aircraft -> airports. Compute assigned_count by counting my_routes rows per (hub, aircraft_shortname). Return list of dicts: {shortname, name, range_km, runway_m, seats_y/j/f, cargo, available_count, current_route_count, config_summary}. Add SQL view v_fleet_availability if it simplifies queries. Handle stopover case: if direct range insufficient, mark eligible_with_stopover=true and include stopover hint.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test with seeded fixture: hub with 3 aircraft (1 fully assigned, 1 partially, 1 free), one ineligible by range, one by runway. Assert correct subset returned with correct available_count.

subtasks.title: Define function signature for get_eligible_aircraft
subtasks.description: Define the Python function signature with type hints and docstring.
subtasks.details: Add docstring, parameter types, return type annotation. Implement logic to join my_fleet -> aircraft -> airports table.

subtasks.title: Implement range and runway checks in get_eligible_aircraft
subtasks.description: Complete the function by adding logic to check range and airport compatibility at both endpoints.
subtasks.details: Use distance_km parameter, query airports for compatible runways, filter aircraft based on conditions.

subtasks.title: Calculate available_count and return aircraft details
subtasks.description: Compute the available_count by subtracting assigned_count from owned_count. Return aircraft data as specified in description.
subtasks.details: Join my_routes to get assigned_count per aircraft, compute available_count, format output.

subtasks.title: Add SQL view v_fleet_availability if beneficial
subtasks.description: Evaluate whether adding a SQL view can simplify queries and add it accordingly.
subtasks.details: Design and implement the SQL view to enhance query performance. Test its impact on overall function efficiency.

subtasks.title: Handle stopover cases in get_eligible_aircraft
subtasks.description: Implement logic to handle scenarios where a direct flight is not possible and mark eligible_with_stopover.
subtasks.details: Update the function to check if a stopover is required and adjust the returned aircraft data accordingly.

tasks.title: API: GET /api/routes/eligible-aircraft
tasks.description: FastAPI endpoint returning JSON list of eligible aircraft for a candidate route. Used by HTMX to populate the dropdown when the user picks origin+destination.
tasks.details: Location: app/api_routes.py. Query params: hub (IATA), dest (IATA). Calls fleet_service.get_eligible_aircraft. Returns 200 with list (possibly empty) plus a reason string when empty (e.g. 'No aircraft at KHI with range >= 6,234 km'). Render via Jinja partial templates/partials/_aircraft_options.html for HTMX swap.
tasks.testStrategy: TestClient: hit endpoint with seeded DB, assert HTML partial contains expected <option> tags and includes range/seats hint text. Empty case asserts the reason message renders.

tasks.title: Frontend: Add Route page with HTMX-driven aircraft dropdown
tasks.description: Page at /routes/add with form fields: origin hub (select, defaults to user's primary), destination (autocomplete or IATA input), aircraft (dropdown populated via HTMX after origin+dest set).
tasks.details: Location: app/templates/pages/add_route.html and route in pages.py. HTMX trigger: hx-get on dest blur/change calls /api/routes/eligible-aircraft with both IATAs, hx-target=#aircraft-select. Each option shows: shortname - name (range km, YxJxF, n more available, currently on m routes). When list is empty, swap in the inline fleet-entry expansion (task 4) instead of an empty select, with the empty-reason message above it.
tasks.testStrategy: Manual: pick KHI -> JFK with a hub that has eligible aircraft (dropdown populates). Pick KHI -> a route no current aircraft can fly (form expands to inline fleet entry). Playwright optional.

tasks.title: Frontend: inline fleet-entry expansion
tasks.description: When no eligible aircraft exist, expand the form in-place to include the my_fleet entry fields (aircraft shortname picker, hub defaulting to route origin, quantity, Y/J/F config, cargo split if applicable, engine, mods: fuel/co2/speed).
tasks.details: Reuse the partial from the My Fleet page (extract templates/partials/_fleet_entry_fields.html if not already). Default hub to route origin and lock it (with override toggle). Quantity default 1. Above the expansion, link to Buy Next page filtered to this hub+route distance so user can sanity-check profitability before committing. Show estimated purchase cost from aircraft table.
tasks.testStrategy: Manual: trigger empty-eligible case, fill fields, verify all required inputs validate client-side before submit is enabled.

tasks.title: Backend: validation service for candidate route
tasks.description: Centralized validator returning structured errors/warnings: range OK, runway OK at both endpoints, seats vs daily demand sanity, duplicate route check, stopover required flag.
tasks.details: Location: app/services/route_validator.py. Function validate_route(hub, dest, aircraft_shortname, config) -> {errors:[], warnings:[], stopover_required:bool, stopover_hint:str|None}. Used by both the dropdown filter (task 1) and the submit endpoint (task 6).
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for each rule: too-short range, runway too short, duplicate (hub,dest,shortname) already in my_routes, demand << seats triggers warning not error.

tasks.title: API: POST /api/routes/add (transactional, supports inline fleet insert)
tasks.description: Single endpoint that accepts route fields plus optional inline fleet payload. Wraps fleet insert + route insert in one SQLite transaction.
tasks.details: Location: app/api_routes.py. Request body: {hub, dest, aircraft_shortname, config{...}, inline_fleet: null | {hub, quantity, config, engine, mods, purchase_price}}. Logic: BEGIN; if inline_fleet: insert/upsert my_fleet row (increment count if same hub+shortname+config exists, else new row); call route_validator; if errors -> ROLLBACK and return 422 with errors; insert my_routes row; COMMIT. Decrement available_count check inside transaction to prevent race. Returns the new route row + a 'needs_extraction_refresh' flag if inline_fleet was used (no route_aircraft rows yet for this config).
tasks.testStrategy: Tests: (a) happy path existing aircraft -> route inserted only; (b) inline fleet path -> both rows inserted, count correct; (c) validator failure -> neither row persisted (assert rollback); (d) duplicate route -> 422.

tasks.title: UX: post-submit success and freshness flag
tasks.description: After successful add, show toast with summary and two CTAs: 'Add another route for this aircraft' (prefills aircraft+hub) and 'Back to routes'. If inline_fleet was used, show a persistent banner on the new route's row: 'Awaiting next extraction for profitability data'.
tasks.details: Location: pages.py redirect target /routes?highlight=<id>&fresh=1. Banner driven by needs_extraction_refresh flag stored on my_routes (add column needs_extraction_refresh BOOLEAN DEFAULT 0; cleared by extraction job when route_aircraft has matching rows for this aircraft+config).
tasks.testStrategy: Manual: add via inline path, verify banner; run extraction; verify banner clears.

tasks.title: Schema migration
tasks.description: Add needs_extraction_refresh column to my_routes; add v_fleet_availability view if introduced in task 1.
tasks.details: Location: app/db/migrations/00X_add_route_with_inline_fleet.sql. Idempotent. Update schema.sql canonical file. Document in README migration section.
tasks.testStrategy: Run on fresh DB and on existing DB copy; assert no data loss and new column defaults correctly.

tasks.title: Cancel/rollback handling on inline form abandonment
tasks.description: Ensure that if the user expands the inline fleet form then cancels or navigates away, no partial state is persisted (form is client-side only until submit).
tasks.details: All inline fleet data lives in the form DOM; no draft saves to server. Add explicit Cancel button that resets the form. Confirm dialog if dirty. No DB writes happen until the single POST in task 6.
tasks.testStrategy: Manual: open form, fill inline fleet, cancel; query my_fleet and my_routes -> no new rows.

tasks.title: Buy Next integration link
tasks.description: From the inline fleet expansion, deep-link to /buy-next/<hub>?distance=<km>&dest=<iata> so the user can verify the aircraft they're about to buy actually ranks well for this route before committing.
tasks.details: Location: add_route.html inline expansion. Opens in new tab. Buy Next page already exists from prior spec; just plumb the query params through (may require small enhancement to filter by distance/dest if not already supported).
tasks.testStrategy: Manual: click link from inline expansion, verify Buy Next loads with correct filters applied.
